title: Incorrect Most Significant Bit Flip in Binary Representation

statement.markdown: You are given a function that attempts to return the most significant bit flip of an integer. The function should return the result of flipping the most significant set bit of the number. For example, the most significant bit of 12 is 3, so 12 flipped would return 11.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a non-negative integer.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the result of flipping the most significant set bit of the input number.
statement.notes: Note that there may be multiple most significant set bits. You should flip the highest one.

examples.explanation: The most significant bit of 8 is 3, so 8 flipped would return 7.

hints.text: You should first find the most significant set bit.
Then flip the most significant set bit.

editorial.title: Most Significant Bit Flip
editorial.intuition: Find the most significant set bit and flip it.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a loop to find the most significant set bit and flip it.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use bitwise operations to find and flip the most significant set bit.
commonMistakes: Misleading the most significant set bit.
